# Prescription Formulary Sample

## Guizhi Decoction

### Composition
Cinnamon Twig 3 liang (peeled), Peony 3 liang, Licorice 2 liang (prepared), Ginger 3 liang (sliced), Jujube 12 pieces (broken)

### Decoction Method
Take the five ingredients, cut three of them, add 7 sheng of water, simmer over low heat to obtain 3 sheng, remove the dregs, adjust to suitable temperature, and take 1 sheng. Soon after taking, drink about 1 sheng of hot rice gruel to aid the medicinal effect, cover with a blanket for about one hour, and it is better to have a slight sweat all over the body; do not let the sweat flow profusely, otherwise the disease will not be cured. If symptoms are relieved after one dose, stop taking the remaining medicine; if not sweating, take another dose according to the previous method; if still not sweating, shorten the interval between doses, taking three doses within half a day. For severe cases, take the medicine day and night, observing the condition continuously; if symptoms persist after one dose, prepare another dose; if no sweating occurs, take up to two or three doses.

### Functions and Indications
Relieves the muscles to expel exterior pathogens, harmonizes nutritive and defensive qi. Indicated for Taiyang wind stroke, with floating yang and weak yin; floating yang causes spontaneous heat, weak yin causes spontaneous sweating. Symptoms include slight aversion to cold, mild aversion to wind, gentle fever, nasal congestion, and retching.

### Monarch-Minister-Assistant-Guide Analysis
- Monarch herb: Cinnamon Twig, acrid and warm, relieves the muscles to expel exterior pathogens, warms and unblocks meridians
- Minister herb: Peony, sour, bitter, and slightly cold, nourishes yin and consolidates nutritive qi
- Assistant herbs: Ginger, acrid and warm, assists Cinnamon Twig in expelling exterior pathogens; Jujube, sweet and warm, supplements qi and nourishes the middle
- Guide herb: Licorice, sweet and warm, harmonizes all herbs

## Mahuang Decoction

### Composition
Ephedra 3 liang (de-jointed), Cinnamon Twig 2 liang (peeled), Licorice 1 liang (prepared), Apricot Kernel 70 pieces (peeled and tipped)

### Decoction Method
Take the four ingredients, add 9 sheng of water, first boil Ephedra to reduce by 2 sheng, remove the froth, add the other ingredients, boil to obtain 2.5 sheng, remove the dregs, warm and take 8 he, cover to induce slight sweating, no need to drink porridge, follow the same aftercare as Guizhi Decoction.

### Functions and Indications
Induces sweating to expel exterior pathogens, ventilates the lungs to relieve asthma. Indicated for Taiyang disease with headache, fever, body pain, low back pain, joint pain, aversion to wind, and absence of sweating with asthma.

### Monarch-Minister-Assistant-Guide Analysis
- Monarch herb: Ephedra, acrid and warm, induces sweating to expel exterior pathogens, ventilates the lungs to relieve asthma
- Minister herb: Cinnamon Twig, acrid and warm, relieves the muscles to expel exterior pathogens, warms and unblocks meridians
- Assistant herb: Apricot Kernel, bitter and warm, descends and benefits lung qi, relieves asthma and stops cough
- Guide herb: Licorice, sweet and warm, harmonizes all herbs

## Xiaochaihu Decoction

### Composition
Bupleurum 8 liang, Scutellaria 3 liang, Ginseng 3 liang, Licorice 3 liang (prepared), Ginger 3 liang (sliced), Jujube 12 pieces (broken), Pinellia 0.5 sheng (washed)

### Decoction Method
Take the seven ingredients, add 12 sheng of water, boil to obtain 6 sheng, remove the dregs, simmer again to obtain 3 sheng, warm and take 1 sheng, three times a day.

### Functions and Indications
Harmonizes Shaoyang. Indicated for cold damage or wind stroke of five to six days, with alternating chills and fever, fullness and discomfort in the chest and hypochondria, reluctance to talk and eat, vexation and propensity to vomit, or chest vexation without vomiting, or thirst, or abdominal pain, or hypochondriac fullness and hardness, or palpitations in the epigastrium and urinary difficulty, or absence of thirst with slight fever, or cough.

### Monarch-Minister-Assistant-Guide Analysis
- Monarch herb: Bupleurum, bitter and neutral, harmonizes Shaoyang, soothes the liver and resolves stagnation
- Minister herb: Scutellaria, bitter and cold, clears heat in Shaoyang
- Assistant herbs: Ginseng, Jujube, and Licorice, supplement qi and strengthen the spleen; Pinellia and Ginger, harmonize the stomach and descend rebellious qi
- Guide herb: Licorice, harmonizes all herbs
